Autobazar123 Overview

One long map of the Autobazar123 app, services, data, protections, tests, and operational surfaces.

Generated from repo on 25 Mar 2026, 21:47
Start Here→Glossary→Architecture→Journeys→Feature Atlas→Coverage Matrix→Change Guides→Routes→API Map→Services→DB Flows→Data Model→Security→External Systems→State Lifecycles→Cron & Jobs→Tests→Fragility & Gaps→Repo Map→Env & Deploy→Troubleshooting→Change Impact→Known Unknowns→
Reading modes hide or reveal technical and debug-heavy blocks without changing the structure.

Autobazar123

Complete Autobazar123 Project Overview

Autobazar123 is a Slovak car marketplace. The app combines discovery, listing detail, seller contact, account flows, dealer/admin operations, and credit-backed paid actions.

Highlighting None
Highlight types: FeatureRouteServiceTableTestGuide
32Page routes
41Route handlers / API endpoints
103Test files and suites
28Database tables
45Migrations
9Tracked fallbacks

Generated from the repo on 25 Mar 2026, 21:47

How To Read This

The atlas is layered so you can start simple and drill into code, services, and tests only when needed.

Confirmed from code and docs

1. Start with Product, Roles, and Glossary

Start with Product, Roles, and Glossary if you want the non-technical view first.

2. Use Architecture, Feature Atlas, and Coverage Matrix to understand how the app is assembled.

Use Architecture, Feature Atlas, and Coverage Matrix to understand how the app is assembled.

3. Use Routes, Services, Data, and Security when you need implementation detail.

Use Routes, Services, Data, and Security when you need implementation detail.

4. Use Change Guides, Tests, Troubleshooting, and Change Impact when you are debugging or planning work.

Use Change Guides, Tests, Troubleshooting, and Change Impact when you are debugging or planning work.

Search filters the atlas items themselves. Use Debug mode when you want the full test, failure, and change-impact detail.

Product And Roles

The easiest mental model is to understand who uses the app and what each role can actually do.

Confirmed from code and docs

Guest

Can browse inventory, view detail pages, use search, read legal pages, and start auth or inquiry flows.

//vysledky/auto/[id]/auth/login/auth/register/kontakt

Authenticated User

Can manage account details, save searches or ads, send inquiries, add listings, and spend credits.

/moj-ucet/ulozene/spravy/pridat-inzerat/kredity

Dealer

Gets dealer-specific listing management, verification, profile pages, and bulk actions on ads.

/dealer/predajca/[slug]/predajcovia

Admin

Can access moderation, logs, settings, analytics, revenue, quality gates, email center, and feature flags.

/admin/nastavenia

Glossary

Short definitions for the terms that keep appearing in the codebase and docs.

Confirmed from code and docs

Next.js

The web framework running the app pages, layouts, API routes, and server rendering.

Route

A URL path such as `/vysledky` or `/api/stripe/checkout`.

API route

A server endpoint inside the app that browser code or background jobs call.

Component

A reusable UI building block, usually a React function under `src/components`.

Supabase

The backend service used for auth, database access, and row-level security.

RLS

Row-level security. Database rules that decide who can read or modify each row.

Algolia

The search engine used for fast listing discovery, filtering, and search suggestions.

Webhook

A server-to-server callback. Stripe uses this to confirm payment events.

Cron

A scheduled background job. Vercel Cron hits protected API routes on a timer.

Idempotency

Protection against applying the same action twice, especially around payments.

Fallback

A degraded path used when a primary service is unavailable. In this repo, fallbacks are tracked and monitored.

Env var

A configuration value like an API key or service URL that is supplied outside the codebase.

Proxy

A request gate in `src/proxy.ts` that applies guardrails like maintenance mode, auth checks, and route handling rules.

Architecture

The app is a Next.js front end and server layer sitting in front of several specialized services. Supabase is the core system of record. Algolia, Stripe, Resend, Cloudflare Images, Upstash, and optional analytics branch off from there.

Confirmed from code and docs

Simple view

The browser talks to the Next.js app. The app renders pages, serves API routes, and coordinates with Supabase for data and auth. Search, payments, emails, media, rate limiting, and analytics are delegated to separate services.

Technical view

Most business logic sits under src/lib, while page shells and route handlers live in src/app. Cron jobs are just protected route handlers triggered by Vercel.

Debug view

When something breaks, the fastest split is: rendering issue, service issue, database or RLS issue, or background job issue.

Journey Maps

These are the main user stories. Each one shows how a real action crosses routes, services, and tests.

Confirmed from code

Search and browse inventory

Guest or buyer Confirmed from code
User starts on the homepage or results page.
Search UI builds URL state and sends search queries through Algolia clients.
If Algolia is unavailable, the app can degrade to a fallback catalog API path.
User opens a listing detail page and can view photos, seller info, and contact actions.

Routes and handlers

/ /vysledky /auto/[id] /api/search/catalog /api/search/count

Services and tests

Services: Algolia, Supabase, Cloudflare Images

  • tests/e2e.test.ts
  • tests/release-gauntlet.test.ts
  • src/lib/algolia/fallback-search.test.ts

Register, login, and recover access

User Confirmed from code
User opens auth pages or the auth modal.
Supabase handles auth state and sessions, including callback and password reset routes.
The app can resend confirmation emails through app API routes and React Email templates.
Google OAuth and optional One Tap can supplement password login.

Routes and handlers

/auth/login /auth/register /auth/reset-password /auth/callback /api/auth/register /api/auth/password-reset

Services and tests

Services: Supabase, Resend, Google Identity Services

  • src/components/AuthModal.email-flow.test.tsx
  • src/lib/auth/password-flow.test.ts
  • src/app/api/auth/password-reset/route.security.test.ts

Create, publish, and manage a listing

User or dealer Confirmed from code
User enters the add-listing wizard and provides vehicle details, photos, and price.
Photo upload uses Cloudflare direct upload and Cloudflare delivery URLs.
Publishing and paid actions rely on Supabase RPCs and credit deductions.
Dashboard and dealer screens let the seller edit, resubmit, top, or mark an ad as sold.

Routes and handlers

/pridat-inzerat /upravit-inzerat/[id] /moje-inzeraty /dealer /api/images/upload-url /api/account/ads

Services and tests

Services: Supabase, Cloudflare Images

  • src/lib/dealer/bulk-actions.test.ts
  • src/app/api/account/ads/mark-sold/route.test.ts
  • src/app/api/account/ads/resubmit/route.test.ts

Buy credits and confirm payment

Authenticated user Confirmed from code
User selects a credit pack on `/kredity`.
The app creates a Stripe Checkout session through a protected API route.
Stripe sends a webhook back to the app after payment completion.
Supabase credit transactions and email notifications are updated server-side.

Routes and handlers

/kredity /kredity/uspech /api/stripe/checkout /api/stripe/webhook

Services and tests

Services: Stripe, Supabase, Resend

  • tests/release-gauntlet.test.ts
  • src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.test.ts
  • src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.idempotency.test.ts

Send inquiries and saved alerts

Buyer and seller Confirmed from code
Buyer submits an inquiry or saves a search/ad.
Supabase stores the inquiry, saved item, or saved-search preference.
Cron jobs can process alerts and queued email jobs.
Admins can inspect email delivery history and quality-gate feeds.

Routes and handlers

/spravy /ulozene /api/inquiries /api/account/saved-searches /api/cron/send-alerts /api/cron/process-email-jobs

Services and tests

Services: Supabase, Resend, Vercel Cron

  • src/lib/inquiries/submit-inquiry.test.ts
  • src/lib/search/saved-searches.test.ts
  • src/app/api/inquiries/route.test.ts

Risk

Coverage Matrix

One row per major feature, showing its main system surfaces and the current curated coverage gap level.

Confirmed from code
FeatureMain routesServicesCore tablesDirect testsGap levelGap note
Medium Good breadth, but `/vysledky` instability and fallback complexity keep this from low-risk coverage.
Medium Core rendering and trust components are covered, but the full detail-page journey has lighter browser depth than search or auth.
Low This area has strong unit and security-focused coverage across auth logic and protected-route behavior.
High Pieces are covered, but there is still no single browser storyline for create -> publish/spend -> sell lifecycle.
Low High-risk area, but direct checkout/webhook/idempotency coverage is already strong.
Medium Core messaging logic is covered well, but cron-driven alert delivery still has lighter direct route coverage.
Medium Important admin routes are covered, but some dashboard truth still depends on external system state and ops wiring.

Change Guides

Fast starting points for the main work areas that tend to span multiple files, services, and tests.

Confirmed from code and docs

Results behavior, URL-state mapping, Algolia config, fallback search, and SEO inventory pages usually move together.

Start here:
  • src/app/(site)/vysledky/AlgoliaSearchPageClient.tsx
  • src/lib/algolia/index.ts
  • src/app/api/search/catalog/route.ts
Main routes:
Services:
Tests to run first:
Core tables:
Common risk: Search regressions can break both buyer UX and SEO-facing discovery paths.

Auth work usually touches entry UI, Supabase session flow, redirects, recovery/reset, and permission-sensitive routes.

Start here:
  • src/components/AuthModal/index.tsx
  • src/lib/auth/password-flow.ts
  • src/app/api/account/password/route.ts
Main routes:
Services:
Tests to run first:
Core tables:
Common risk: Small redirect or session mistakes can lock users out or weaken protected flows.

Credit-pack UI, checkout metadata, webhook verification, balance mutation, and payment emails all need to stay aligned.

Start here:
  • src/app/(site)/kredity/CreditsPageClient.tsx
  • src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts
  • src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts
Main routes:
Services:
Tests to run first:
Core tables:
Common risk: This is release-critical. A regression can mis-credit users or break paid seller actions.

Seller flow work usually spans wizard UI, validation, photo upload, credit-backed actions, and downstream ad state.

Start here:
  • src/app/(site)/pridat-inzerat/AdWizardClient.tsx
  • src/app/api/images/upload-url/route.ts
  • src/lib/validation/listings.ts
Main routes:
Services:
Tests to run first:
Core tables:
Common risk: This area is still the easiest place to create multi-step seller regressions.

Admin work usually touches dashboard UI, server actions, RBAC checks, moderation data, and quality-gate ingestion.

Start here:
  • src/app/(site)/admin/AdminDashboardClient.tsx
  • src/app/(site)/admin/actions.ts
  • src/app/api/admin/quality-gates/route.ts
Main routes:
Services:
Tests to run first:
Core tables:
Common risk: Admin mistakes have a high blast radius because they combine privilege, ops visibility, and moderation actions.

Service Atlas

This is the dependency map: why each service exists, where it is used, and what would hurt if it failed.

Confirmed from code and docs
App coreConfirmed from code

Drives pages, layouts, route handlers, server rendering, and metadata files.

Used by: All user-facing and API surfaces.

Env vars: NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL

  • src/app
  • src/components
  • src/proxy.ts

Tests:

Failure impact: If page rendering or route handlers fail, the whole product is directly affected.

Data, auth, RBACConfirmed from code and docs

Handles auth, database persistence, row-level security, server/admin clients, and many seller/admin flows.

Used by: Auth, listings, credits, inquiries, saved items, admin, emails, analytics.

Env vars: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_REDIRECT_ORIGIN

  • src/lib/supabase/server.ts
  • src/lib/supabase/client.ts
  • src/lib/auth/rbac.ts
  • supabase/migrations

Tests:

Failure impact: Supabase is the most central dependency. Auth, persistence, and protected reads or writes all depend on it.

SearchConfirmed from code and docs

Provides low-latency listing search, faceting, sorting, and query-driven results pages.

Used by: Homepage suggestions, results page, SEO inventory logic, search sync jobs.

Env vars: NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_APP_ID, NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_SEARCH_KEY, ALGOLIA_ADMIN_KEY, ALGOLIA_SYNC_SECRET

  • src/lib/algolia/index.ts
  • src/app/(site)/vysledky/AlgoliaSearchPageClient.tsx
  • src/app/api/algolia/sync/route.ts

Tests:

Failure impact: Search quality depends on Algolia configuration, index sync, and the monitored fallback path.

PaymentsConfirmed from code and docs

Creates checkout sessions and confirms payment completion through webhook events.

Used by: Credit purchases, admin revenue reporting, payment notifications.

Env vars: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET

  • src/lib/stripe/client.ts
  • src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts
  • src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts

Tests:

Failure impact: Payment correctness, credit balances, and confirmation emails depend on this flow staying exact.

Outbound emailConfirmed from code and docs

Sends auth emails, payment emails, moderation emails, saved-search alerts, and queued jobs.

Used by: Auth, payment confirmation, alerts, moderation, admin email center.

Env vars: RESEND_API_KEY, EMAIL_FROM, EMAIL_REPLY_TO

  • src/lib/email/transactional-email.ts
  • src/lib/email/react-email-templates.tsx
  • src/lib/email/jobs.ts

Tests:

Failure impact: Email delivery is operationally important for auth and alerts, but failures should stay visible rather than silent.

MediaConfirmed from code and docs

Handles direct upload URLs, image delivery, and transform-based optimization for listing photos.

Used by: Listing wizard, detail pages, dashboards, dealer pages, homepage cards.

Env vars: CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID, CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN, CLOUDFLARE_IMAGES_REQUIRE_SIGNED_URLS

  • src/app/api/images/upload-url/route.ts
  • src/utils/upload.ts
  • src/lib/image-optimizer.ts

Tests:

Failure impact: Photo upload and quality are critical for marketplace trust and listing conversion.

Rate limitingConfirmed from code and docs

Provides infrastructure for rate limiting and strict route protection.

Used by: Sensitive auth, contact, and checkout flows.

Env vars: UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL, UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN

  • src/lib/ratelimit.ts
  • scripts/check-prod-rate-limit-env.mjs

Tests:

Failure impact: Missing production credentials are treated as release-blocking because strict routes fail closed.

MeasurementConfirmed from code and docs

Captures first-party analytics events and can forward to GA4 or PostHog when configured and consent allows it.

Used by: Core product analytics, operator dashboards, quality and KPI reporting.

Env vars: NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID, NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST

  • src/lib/analytics/events.ts
  • src/lib/analytics/client.ts
  • src/app/api/analytics/events/route.ts

Tests:

Failure impact: Analytics is less critical than auth or payments, but still important for product visibility and experimentation.

OperationsConfirmed from code and docs

Runs scheduled API routes and hosts the production app build and env configuration.

Used by: Alert sending, email job processing, sold cleanup, ad expiration, deployment gates.

Env vars: CRON_SECRET, QUALITY_GATE_ALERT_ALLOWED_REPOSITORIES, QUALITY_GATE_ALERT_OIDC_AUDIENCE

  • src/app/api/cron/send-alerts/route.ts
  • src/app/api/cron/process-email-jobs/route.ts
  • .github/workflows

Tests:

Failure impact: Misconfigured secrets or failed jobs can silently degrade alerts, cleanup, or monitoring unless surfaced.

Auth convenienceConfirmed from code and docs

Supports Google OAuth and optional One Tap user entry points.

Used by: Auth modal and provider initialization.

Env vars: NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_REDIRECT_ORIGIN

  • src/components/GoogleOneTap.tsx
  • src/components/AuthModal/useAuthModal.ts

Tests:

Failure impact: Helpful but secondary. Misconfiguration mainly affects sign-in convenience and redirect correctness.

Database Relationships And Write Map

This is the business-level data graph: which tables are central, how they relate, and which API routes read or write them.

Confirmed from code
Confirmed from code and docs

Profiles is the account anchor. Most user-owned rows attach here before they branch into listings, messages, saved state, or credits.

Confirmed from code and docs

Dealer records extend an account into a public seller identity with verification and dashboard behavior layered on top.

Confirmed from code and docs

Ads is the marketplace center. Discovery, detail pages, alerts, inquiries, moderation, and paid seller actions all orbit this table.

Confirmed from code

Saved searches capture buyer intent. Cron compares these filters against newer ads and can trigger alert delivery.

Confirmed from code and docs

Credit history ties payment confirmation to spendable balance and paid listing actions such as publishing or featuring.

Confirmed from code

Inquiry rows connect buyers and sellers around one listing and power the messaging surface.

Confirmed from code

Listing reports create the moderation trail from community input into admin review and action history.

Confirmed from code

Queued email jobs hold deferred outbound work so alerts and transactional sends can be retried and inspected operationally.

API read and write map for core tables

TableFeature areasRelated tablesWritten by API routesRead by API routes
None detected None detected
None detected
None detected None detected
None detected
None detected
None detected

Tracking rows for taxonomy import or sync operations.supabase/migrations/20260319113000_add_vehicle_taxonomy_sync_metadata.sql

Security And Reliability

The product is built around trust: correct auth, correct payments, correct listing visibility, and visible degraded-operation handling.

Confirmed from code and docs
Confirmed from code and docs

RLS and server-side RBAC

Protected reads and writes rely on Supabase row-level policies plus explicit site-admin checks in server code.

  • supabase/migrations
  • src/lib/auth/rbac.ts
  • src/lib/auth/site-admin.ts
Confirmed from code and docs

Stripe signature verification and idempotency

Webhooks are verified and payment side effects are guarded against duplicates or replay.

  • src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts
  • src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.idempotency.test.ts
  • supabase/migrations/20260213000200_idempotency_keys.sql
Confirmed from code and docs

Rate limiting and fail-closed posture

Sensitive routes use strict limiter behavior and production env guards to avoid shipping without required Redis credentials.

  • src/lib/ratelimit.ts
  • scripts/check-prod-rate-limit-env.mjs
Confirmed from code

CSRF, CSP, maintenance unlock, and request origin checks

The app includes request hardening utilities to reduce token misuse, unsafe embeds, and untrusted maintenance access.

  • src/lib/security/csrf.ts
  • src/lib/security/csp.ts
  • src/lib/security/maintenance-bypass.ts
  • src/lib/auth/request-origin.ts
Confirmed from code and docs

Fallback governance

Fallbacks are treated as degraded operation, not success. They carry owners, thresholds, and review dates.

  • src/lib/fallbacks/registry.ts
  • src/lib/fallbacks/monitor.ts
Confirmed from code and docs

Cron secret enforcement and CI posture checks

Scheduled routes and GitHub-driven monitoring use explicit secret or OIDC checks before doing work.

  • src/lib/cron/route-helpers.ts
  • .github/workflows/release-security-gate.yml
  • scripts/github-actions-oidc-posture.mjs
This atlas detected 9 registered fallback policies in src/lib/fallbacks/registry.ts.

External Systems And Out-Of-Repo Setup

This is where Git stops being the whole truth. These systems are required at runtime, but part of their setup lives in dashboards, DNS, or provider consoles.

Confirmed from code and docs
Supabase dashboardConfirmed from code and docs

Supabase project and Auth console

Database, auth sessions, redirect allow-list, RLS-backed product data.

Outside repo:
  • Redirect allow-list for local and production auth callbacks.
  • Project URL, anon key, and service-role key management.
  • Storage/auth console settings that are not fully represented in Git.
Env vars:
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URLNEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEYSUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEYNEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_REDIRECT_ORIGIN
Stripe dashboardConfirmed from code and docs

Stripe dashboard and webhook endpoint

Credit-pack checkout, webhook delivery, and revenue visibility.

Outside repo:
  • Live products/prices and the checkout configuration they imply.
  • Webhook registration pointed at `/api/stripe/webhook`.
  • Webhook signing secret rotation and monitoring.
Env vars:
STRIPE_SECRET_KEYSTRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
Vercel dashboardConfirmed from code and docs

Vercel project and cron scheduler

Production hosting, env vars, domain wiring, and scheduled route execution.

Outside repo:
  • Project-level env vars for all services.
  • Cron schedule wiring for `/api/cron/*` routes.
  • Deployment state, domains, and preview/production separation.
Env vars:
CRON_SECRETQUALITY_GATE_ALERT_ALLOWED_REPOSITORIESQUALITY_GATE_ALERT_OIDC_AUDIENCE
Resend dashboard + DNSConfirmed from code and docs

Resend sender domain

Auth, alert, and payment email delivery.

Outside repo:
  • Verified sending domain and DNS records.
  • Sender identity and reply-to behavior.
  • Operational deliverability outside the codebase.
Env vars:
RESEND_API_KEYEMAIL_FROMEMAIL_REPLY_TO
Algolia dashboardConfirmed from code and docs

Algolia application and indices

Fast results pages, filtering, ranking, and search sync.

Outside repo:
  • Application credentials and per-environment indices.
  • Ranking/faceting settings that influence results quality.
  • Sync expectations between Supabase ads and Algolia records.
Env vars:
NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_APP_IDNEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_SEARCH_KEYALGOLIA_ADMIN_KEYALGOLIA_SYNC_SECRET
Upstash dashboardConfirmed from code and docs

Upstash Redis instance

Strict rate limiting on sensitive endpoints.

Outside repo:
  • REST URL and token lifecycle.
  • Environment-specific Redis instances.
  • Operational visibility when strict routes fail closed.
Env vars:
UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URLUPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN
Cloudflare dashboardConfirmed from code and docs

Cloudflare Images delivery config

Listing photo upload and optimized delivery.

Outside repo:
  • Account permissions and API token scope.
  • Image delivery/signing posture.
  • Any custom delivery-domain decisions.
Env vars:
CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_IDCLOUDFLARE_API_TOKENCLOUDFLARE_IMAGES_REQUIRE_SIGNED_URLS
Cloudflare dashboardInferred / needs external verification

Cloudflare Turnstile

Public abuse protection on inquiry and report-style forms.

Outside repo:
  • Site key and secret registration.
  • Allowed domains and challenge posture.
  • Monitoring on public anti-abuse flow.
Env vars:
NEXT_PUBLIC_TURNSTILE_SITE_KEYTURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY
VINCARIO dashboardConfirmed from code and docs

VINCARIO VIN decode account

VIN decode assistance in seller flows.

Outside repo:
  • API credential issuance and quota.
  • Provider-side response and rate-limit behavior.
  • Any production allow-listing outside the repo.
Env vars:
VINCARIO_API_KEY
Supabase Auth + Google Cloud consoleConfirmed from code and docs

Google identity / OAuth configuration

Google login and optional One Tap convenience.

Outside repo:
  • Google OAuth app registration.
  • Allowed origins and callback setup.
  • Any One Tap domain and consent behavior.
Env vars:
NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_REDIRECT_ORIGIN

State Lifecycles

These mini-maps show how important things move through the system over time, not just where the code lives.

Confirmed from code

Listing lifecycle

Confirmed from code

How a seller listing moves from creation into its visible marketplace states.

Draft→ Pending review→ Active→ Sold or expired

Routes

Tables

Tests

Credit purchase lifecycle

Confirmed from code and docs

How a user buys credits and how the balance is updated only after verified server-side confirmation.

Pack selected→ Checkout session created→ Stripe payment completed→ Webhook verified→ Credits + notifications recorded

Routes

Tables

Tests

Inquiry lifecycle

Confirmed from code

How a buyer message is created, stored, and updated through the messaging flow.

Submitted→ Visible in conversation→ Seen or updated→ Closed in practice

Routes

Tables

Tests

Saved-search alert lifecycle

Confirmed from code

How discovery preferences turn into later reminder or alert behavior.

Search saved→ Preference stored→ Cron scans fresh ads→ Alert sent or queued→ Last notified pointer updated

Routes

Tables

Tests

Email job lifecycle

Confirmed from code

How deferred outbound email work is queued, claimed, and resolved.

Queued→ Claimed by cron→ Sent→ Failed and visible to operators

Routes

Tables

Tests

Cron And Background Jobs

Background work is implemented as protected API routes. Vercel Cron or internal ops triggers these routes with a shared secret.

Confirmed from code

Cron route to clean up sold-listing state.

Source: src/app/api/cron/cleanup-sold/route.ts

Cron route to expire stale ads and tidy related search state.

Source: src/app/api/cron/expire-ads/route.ts

Cron route for email job queue processing.

Source: src/app/api/cron/process-email-jobs/route.ts

Cron route for marketplace alerts.

Source: src/app/api/cron/send-alerts/route.ts

Every Test File And What It Protects

Test fileLayerAreaWhat it doesRun withSample titles
Tooling / policyPlatform & utilitiesvalidateSuite accepts valid suite | createReportTemplate creates all task entries | scoreReport computes weighted score and gradenode --test scripts/agent-benchmark.test.mjsvalidateSuite accepts valid suite | createReportTemplate creates all task entries | scoreReport computes weighted score and grade | scoreReport fails on unknown task id
Tooling / policyPlatform & utilitiesvalidateAgentContract accepts valid contract | validateAgentContract fails when low fallback is missingnode --test scripts/agent-contract-check.test.mjsvalidateAgentContract accepts valid contract | validateAgentContract fails when low fallback is missing
Tooling / policyPlatform & utilitiesparseArgs reads supported flags | resolveThreadId prefers explicit arg and falls back to CODEX_THREAD_ID | findSessionFileByThreadId locates the matching session lognode --test scripts/current-chat-model.test.mjsparseArgs reads supported flags | resolveThreadId prefers explicit arg and falls back to CODEX_THREAD_ID | findSessionFileByThreadId locates the matching session log | readSessionDetails returns the latest turn_context model | getCurrentChatModel reads the current chat thread from env
Tooling / policyPlatform & utilitieseasy ops exports callable functionnode --test scripts/easy-ops.test.mjseasy ops exports callable function
Tooling / policyPlatform & utilitiesparseSemver parses stable versions and rejects invalid input | compareSemver compares version precedence | evaluateFrameworkPatchPosture passes when installed versions are currentnode --test scripts/framework-patch-posture.test.mjsparseSemver parses stable versions and rejects invalid input | compareSemver compares version precedence | evaluateFrameworkPatchPosture passes when installed versions are current | evaluateFrameworkPatchPosture fails when patch lag exceeds grace window | evaluateFrameworkPatchPosture warns on major upgrades and enforces react parity
Tooling / policySecurity & reliabilityevaluateGitHubActionsOidcPosture passes with required workflow markers | evaluateGitHubActionsOidcPosture reports missing markersnode --test scripts/github-actions-oidc-posture.test.mjsevaluateGitHubActionsOidcPosture passes with required workflow markers | evaluateGitHubActionsOidcPosture reports missing markers
Tooling / policyPlatform & utilitiesshouldEnforceProdRateLimitEnv enforces for production targets | findMissingRequiredEnvVars detects missing vars | runProdRateLimitEnvGuard skips outside production targetsnode --test scripts/check-prod-rate-limit-env.test.mjsshouldEnforceProdRateLimitEnv enforces for production targets | findMissingRequiredEnvVars detects missing vars | runProdRateLimitEnvGuard skips outside production targets | runProdRateLimitEnvGuard fails when production vars are missing | runProdRateLimitEnvGuard passes when all production vars exist
Tooling / policyUI, accessibility & journeysparseArgs supports headed, fail-on-issues, and base-url | shouldKeepChromeLogEntry keeps violation and warning entries | shouldKeepChromeLogEntry drops ignored noisenode --test scripts/chrome-console-quick-check.test.mjsparseArgs supports headed, fail-on-issues, and base-url | shouldKeepChromeLogEntry keeps violation and warning entries | shouldKeepChromeLogEntry drops ignored noise | post-login continue selector does not match social Google CTA text | Continue | Pokracovat
Tooling / policyLocalization & brandvalidateCatalogContracts passes when locale catalogs are aligned | validateCatalogContracts reports missing keys | validateCatalogContracts reports placeholder mismatchnode --test scripts/i18n-catalog-contract.test.mjsvalidateCatalogContracts passes when locale catalogs are aligned | validateCatalogContracts reports missing keys | validateCatalogContracts reports placeholder mismatch | validateCatalogContracts reports empty and padded values
Tooling / policyLocalization & brandstripDiacritics and hasDiacritics work | normalizeLookupKey strips accents and lowercases by locale | normalizeManualDictionary keeps only valid diacritic pairsnode --test scripts/i18n-locale-diacritics-check.test.mjsstripDiacritics and hasDiacritics work | normalizeLookupKey strips accents and lowercases by locale | normalizeManualDictionary keeps only valid diacritic pairs | flattenCatalog returns nested text entries | deriveDictionary identifies accented canonical forms and ambiguities | findMissingDiacritics reports plain words that exist in dictionary
Tooling / policyPlatform & utilitiesnormalizeUrl strips tracking params and normalizes host/path | parseLinksMarkdown maps entries to TODO and DONE sections | parseLinksMarkdown extracts URL token from annotated linesnode --test scripts/links-ingest.test.mjsnormalizeUrl strips tracking params and normalizes host/path | parseLinksMarkdown maps entries to TODO and DONE sections | parseLinksMarkdown extracts URL token from annotated lines | buildSnapshot tracks duplicates across sections
Tooling / policyAnalytics & performanceparsePerformanceBudgetArgs parses cli flags | percentile calculates interpolated values | buildPerformanceSnapshot aggregates p95 metricsnode --test scripts/performance-budget-gate.test.mjsparsePerformanceBudgetArgs parses cli flags | percentile calculates interpolated values | buildPerformanceSnapshot aggregates p95 metrics | evaluatePerformanceBudgetPolicy catches threshold and regression failures
Tooling / policySecurity & reliabilityevaluateSecurityPolicy returns no errors for satisfied policy | evaluateSecurityPolicy reports missing markersnode --test scripts/security-release-gate.test.mjsevaluateSecurityPolicy returns no errors for satisfied policy | evaluateSecurityPolicy reports missing markers
Tooling / policyPlatform & utilitiesvalidateSkillGraph passes when links resolve | validateSkillGraph reports missing targetsnode --test scripts/skill-graph-check.test.mjsvalidateSkillGraph passes when links resolve | validateSkillGraph reports missing targets
Tooling / policyLocalization & brandfindMissingDiacritics reports missing accents in code string literals | findMissingDiacritics ignores non-literal identifiers in code files | findMissingDiacritics reports missing accents in JSX text nodesnode --test scripts/slovak-diacritics-check.test.mjsfindMissingDiacritics reports missing accents in code string literals | findMissingDiacritics ignores non-literal identifiers in code files | findMissingDiacritics reports missing accents in JSX text nodes | findMissingDiacritics skips URL and slug fragments | applyFixes rewrites plain text and preserves simple capitalization | applyFixes repairs words that are only partially accented
Tooling / policyUI, accessibility & journeysparseUiQualityArgs parses flags | buildUiQualitySteps filters sitewide and unit stepsnode --test scripts/ui-quality-gate.test.mjsparseUiQualityArgs parses flags | buildUiQualitySteps filters sitewide and unit steps
Unit / integrationAccounts, messaging & adminPOST /api/account/ads/mark-sold | marks an active ad as sold and returns sale confirmation metadata | rejects marking a non-active ad as soldnpm run test:unitPOST /api/account/ads/mark-sold | marks an active ad as sold and returns sale confirmation metadata | rejects marking a non-active ad as sold
Unit / integrationAccounts, messaging & adminPOST /api/account/ads/resubmit | resubmits a rejected ad back to pending | rejects resubmitting a non-rejected adnpm run test:unitPOST /api/account/ads/resubmit | resubmits a rejected ad back to pending | rejects resubmitting a non-rejected ad
Unit / integrationAuthparseRecoveryPasswordBody | returns the parsed body for a valid recovery request | rejects missing or short password payloadsnpm run test:unitparseRecoveryPasswordBody | returns the parsed body for a valid recovery request | rejects missing or short password payloads | rejects missing token hashes
Unit / integrationAuthPOST /api/account/phone security | returns 429 for abuse throttling before profile mutation | blocks cross-user style payload tampering via strict schemanpm run test:unitPOST /api/account/phone security | returns 429 for abuse throttling before profile mutation | blocks cross-user style payload tampering via strict schema | always scopes mutation to authenticated user id
Unit / integrationAccounts, messaging & adminadmin quality gates parser | parses valid webapp audit summary payload | returns null for malformed payloadnpm run test:unitadmin quality gates parser | parses valid webapp audit summary payload | returns null for malformed payload | parses quality gate failure log rows | returns only active alerts after collapse | derives active alerts from live github workflow statuses
Unit / integrationAuthPOST /api/auth/password-reset security | returns 429 for abuse throttling before provider calls | rejects cross-origin requests before provider callsnpm run test:unitPOST /api/auth/password-reset security | returns 429 for abuse throttling before provider calls | rejects cross-origin requests before provider calls | rejects requests without a CSRF token before provider calls | stays enumeration-safe for unknown accounts | does not leak provider internals in non-enumeration errors
Unit / integrationAccounts, messaging & admingetContactSubmitRateLimitIdentifier | uses stable request fingerprinting for contact submit throttlingnpm run test:unitgetContactSubmitRateLimitIdentifier | uses stable request fingerprinting for contact submit throttling
Unit / integrationPlatform & utilitiesGET /api/flags cache boundaries | returns user-specific flags with private no-store cache headers | keeps private no-store cache headers when flag resolution failsnpm run test:unitGET /api/flags cache boundaries | returns user-specific flags with private no-store cache headers | keeps private no-store cache headers when flag resolution fails
Unit / integrationAccounts, messaging & adminPATCH /api/inquiries | marks an inquiry as qualified for the ad seller | rejects qualification update when current user does not own the adnpm run test:unitPATCH /api/inquiries | marks an inquiry as qualified for the ad seller | rejects qualification update when current user does not own the ad
Unit / integrationAccounts, messaging & adminPOST /api/listing-reports | stores a report for an active ad | rejects reporting your own adnpm run test:unitPOST /api/listing-reports | stores a report for an active ad | rejects reporting your own ad | returns duplicate success when the same reporter already has an open report

Browser / scenarioUI, accessibility & journeyswebapp auditnpm run audit:webappwebapp audit

Fragility And Test Gaps

This section separates two things: what already looks brittle, and what still looks under-proven.

Confirmed from code and docs
Observed in local verification on 25 March 2026

Results page browser instability

The results page remains one of the most behavior-heavy surfaces: search state, routing, filters, and async rendering all meet there.

Evidence: Observed in local verification on 25 March 2026: `npm run test:web-interface` timed out on `/vysledky`, and `npm run test:mobile-matrix` hit one related landmark wait failure there.

Tests:
  • tests/web-interface-guidelines.test.ts
  • tests/release-gauntlet.test.ts
Observed in local verification on 25 March 2026

Accessibility contrast debt across key pages

Several core routes still fail automated color-contrast checks, which means UI polish and accessibility debt are overlapping today.

Evidence: Observed in local verification on 25 March 2026: `npm run test:a11y` and `npm run test:mobile-matrix` flagged contrast failures on `/`, `/vysledky`, `/auth/login`, `/auth/register`, `/kredity`, `/zmluva`, and `/kontakt`.

Tests:
  • tests/accessibility-gate.test.ts
  • tests/reflow-zoom.test.ts
Confirmed from code and docs

Search depends on coordinated fallback behavior

Search is fast because Algolia is central, but that also means search correctness depends on index sync, client config, URL-state mapping, and monitored fallback behavior staying aligned.

Evidence: Confirmed from code and docs: search uses Algolia, an API fallback catalog route, and fallback governance telemetry.

Tests:
  • src/lib/algolia/fallback-search.test.ts
  • src/lib/algolia/route-sync.test.ts
  • tests/release-gauntlet.test.ts
Confirmed from code and docs

Credits and payments have high blast radius

The Stripe flow is well protected, but if it regresses the impact is immediate: credit balances, paid actions, and user trust all suffer together.

Evidence: Confirmed from code and docs: checkout, webhook verification, idempotency, and credit mutation are tightly coupled.

Tests:
  • src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.test.ts
  • src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.idempotency.test.ts
  • src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.rate-limit.test.ts
Inferred / needs external verification

External dashboard truth is still partly outside Git

Some runtime behavior depends on dashboard state we cannot fully diff or review in the repo alone.

Evidence: Known repo gap: Stripe products/webhooks, Resend domain status, some Supabase console settings, and final Vercel cron timing require external confirmation.

Tests:
  • scripts/check-prod-rate-limit-env.test.mjs
  • scripts/security-release-gate.test.mjs
Confirmed from code

Cron routes

The cron handlers are important, but the repo currently shows much stronger indirect coverage than direct route-level tests for send-alerts, process-email-jobs, expire-ads, and cleanup-sold.

Why it matters: Background failures are easy to miss because the user does not trigger them manually in the UI.

Confirmed from code

VIN decode and taxonomy APIs

The repo currently shows little or no direct automated coverage for the VIN decode path and public vehicle taxonomy route.

Why it matters: These integrations depend on external data contracts and are easier to break silently.

Inferred from code structure

End-to-end seller lifecycle

There is coverage around pieces of listing management, but not one obvious browser storyline that follows create listing -> publish/spend credits -> resubmit or mark sold.

Why it matters: The seller journey crosses UI, media, auth, credits, and listing-state changes.

Confirmed from code and docs

External configuration verification

Repo tests can assert code posture and env presence, but they cannot fully prove third-party dashboard state such as webhook registration, DNS verification, or provider quotas.

Why it matters: Production can still fail even when local code and unit tests are green.

Repo Map And Commands

This is the codebase shape. The commands listed here are the most useful entry points for development and verification.

Confirmed from code and docs

src/app

Next.js pages, layouts, route handlers, metadata routes, and top-level app behavior.

src/components

UI components, client flows, and route-level interaction building blocks.

src/lib

Domain logic and adapters grouped by concern such as auth, search, email, or security.

src/config

Business constants and site configuration values.

src/context

React contexts such as auth/session state.

src/i18n

Locale config and translation catalogs.

supabase

Migrations, database posture, and SQL tests.

tests

Browser-level Playwright suites and smoke-style checks.

scripts

Repo tooling, audits, guards, imports, and maintenance helpers.

docs

Project playbooks, checklists, and generated atlas output.

Useful commands

  • npm run dev → next dev
  • npm run build → npm run check:prod-rate-limit-env && next build
  • npm run lint → eslint && npm run check:text-encoding && npm run check:sk-diacritics && npm run check:i18n-contract && npm run check:i18n-diacritics && npm run check:theme-guard
  • npm run test:unit → vitest run
  • npm run test:e2e → npx playwright test tests/e2e.test.ts
  • npm run test:web-interface → npx playwright test tests/web-interface-guidelines.test.ts tests/web-interface-sitewide.test.ts
  • npm run test:a11y → npx playwright test tests/accessibility-gate.test.ts tests/reflow-zoom.test.ts
  • npm run test:keyboard → npx playwright test tests/keyboard-navigation.test.ts
  • npm run test:mobile-matrix → npx playwright test tests/accessibility-gate.test.ts tests/reflow-zoom.test.ts --project=mobile-pixel-7 --project=mobile-iphone-13-landscape
  • npm run test:release-gauntlet → npx playwright test tests/release-gauntlet.test.ts
  • npm run test:db:rls → node scripts/supabase-db-tests.mjs
  • npm run audit:chrome-console → node scripts/chrome-console-quick-check.mjs
  • npm run check:prod-rate-limit-env → node scripts/check-prod-rate-limit-env.mjs
  • npm run test:security:release-gate → node scripts/security-release-gate.mjs
  • npm run links:ingest → node scripts/links-ingest.mjs

Env And Deployment

Env vars explain a lot of the invisible wiring. This view shows which services depend on external configuration and what part of the app they power.

Confirmed from code and docs

Next.js App Router

All user-facing and API surfaces.

NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URLNEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL

Supabase

Auth, listings, credits, inquiries, saved items, admin, emails, analytics.

NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URLNEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEYSUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEYNEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_REDIRECT_ORIGIN

Algolia

Homepage suggestions, results page, SEO inventory logic, search sync jobs.

NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_APP_IDNEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_SEARCH_KEYALGOLIA_ADMIN_KEYALGOLIA_SYNC_SECRET

Stripe

Credit purchases, admin revenue reporting, payment notifications.

STRIPE_SECRET_KEYSTRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET

Resend + React Email

Auth, payment confirmation, alerts, moderation, admin email center.

RESEND_API_KEYEMAIL_FROMEMAIL_REPLY_TO

Cloudflare Images

Listing wizard, detail pages, dashboards, dealer pages, homepage cards.

CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_IDCLOUDFLARE_API_TOKENCLOUDFLARE_IMAGES_REQUIRE_SIGNED_URLS

Upstash Redis

Sensitive auth, contact, and checkout flows.

UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URLUPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN

Analytics transports

Core product analytics, operator dashboards, quality and KPI reporting.

NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_IDNEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEYNEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_HOST

Vercel Cron + deployment

Alert sending, email job processing, sold cleanup, ad expiration, deployment gates.

CRON_SECRETQUALITY_GATE_ALERT_ALLOWED_REPOSITORIESQUALITY_GATE_ALERT_OIDC_AUDIENCE

Google Identity Services

Auth modal and provider initialization.

NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_REDIRECT_ORIGIN
Production deployment posture also depends on external systems that are not fully visible in Git: Vercel settings, Stripe dashboard setup, Supabase console config, and Resend domain verification.

Troubleshooting Atlas

If something breaks, use this as the fast map from symptom to likely subsystem, file area, and tests to run first.

Confirmed from code and docs

Search looks broken or empty

  • Check Algolia keys and the results page runtime.
  • Inspect `src/lib/algolia/index.ts` and fallback monitoring entries.
  • Run `npm run test:e2e`, `npm run test:release-gauntlet`, and `npm run test:unit` for Algolia-related modules.

Login or registration is failing

  • Check Supabase auth configuration and redirect allow-list.
  • Inspect auth modal flow, callback route, and password reset endpoints.
  • Run auth-focused unit tests plus `npm run test:release-gauntlet`.

Credits or checkout are wrong

  • Inspect Stripe checkout route, webhook route, and credit transaction data.
  • Confirm webhook secret and session metadata.
  • Run Stripe unit tests and the release gauntlet.

Photos fail to upload or look wrong

  • Check Cloudflare credentials and upload URL route behavior.
  • Inspect image validation rules and optimizer usage.
  • Run upload and image-optimizer unit tests.

Alerts or transactional emails are missing

  • Inspect queued jobs, Resend config, and email-delivery tables.
  • Check cron routes for alert or email job processing.
  • Run email template tests and verify cron secrets.

Admin page or moderation tools are inaccessible

  • Verify site-admin entitlements and RBAC checks.
  • Inspect `/admin` redirect behavior and admin quality-gate routes.
  • Run release gauntlet and admin route tests.

Change Impact Map

This section shows how broad a change usually becomes once you touch a high-value part of the app.

Confirmed from code and docs

Search

Touches results UI, Algolia client config, URL state mapping, fallback search, SEO inventory pages, sync jobs, and multiple browser suites.

Auth

Touches Supabase session flows, callback redirects, auth UI, email delivery, security helpers, and protected-route tests.

Payments

Touches checkout route, webhook processing, Supabase credit RPCs, email confirmations, admin revenue reporting, and release-critical tests.

Listing wizard and photos

Touches validation, upload URLs, Cloudflare media behavior, dashboard edit flows, and seller-side controls.

Admin and moderation

Touches RBAC, quality-gate ingestion, moderation tables, logs, feature flags, and protected route behavior.

Emails and alerts

Touches templates, delivery adapters, cron processing, saved-search logic, and admin email center visibility.

Known Unknowns

This atlas is repo-backed, but not every production truth lives in Git.

Inferred / needs external verification

External verification note

Third-party console configuration is only partially visible from the repo. Stripe dashboard products, webhook registrations, Resend domain setup, and some Supabase console settings still need external verification.

External verification note

Cron schedules are partly documented in code and docs, but final production timing belongs to Vercel configuration outside this file.

External verification note

Some route-purpose descriptions are inferred from file names when the route is not described in docs.
